faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about the competition

dates.question: What are the dates of the 2026 competition?
dates.answer: The 2026 edition of the SUMI JO International Singing Competition will take place from July 5 to 11, 2026 at Château de La Ferté-Imbault, in the Sologne region. This exceptional week will include preliminary auditions, semi-finals, the grand finale, as well as concerts, masterclasses, and cultural events. The detailed program will be announced in spring 2026.

location.question: Where does the competition take place?
location.answer: The competition is held at Château de La Ferté-Imbault, a thousand-year-old estate with foundations dating back to 980 AD, located in the Loire Valley less than two hours from Paris. This exceptional historic monument, both an imposing fortress and an elegant château, offers a unique romantic and inspiring setting. A perfect venue to host this international artistic event.

tickets.question: How can I buy tickets to attend the competition?
tickets.answer: Online ticket sales will open on March 1, 2026. You will be able to reserve tickets for all events: auditions, semi-finals, grand finale, and gala concerts. As seats are limited and demand is high, we recommend signing up now for our email notification to be alerted when sales begin and benefit from priority access.

ticketing.label: TICKETS
ticketing.success: Notification registered! You will be alerted on March 1st.
ticketing.error: Please enter a valid email address
ticketing.alreadyRegistered: This email address is already registered
ticketing.errorGeneric: An error occurred. Please try again.

introduction.title: An Exceptional Edition
introduction.content1: The 2026 edition of the SUMI JO International Singing Competition takes place in a doubly exceptional context. This year marks the celebration of 40 years of Sumi Jo's international career, the world-renowned Korean soprano whose crystalline voice and virtuosity have conquered the greatest opera stages.
introduction.content2: Simultaneously, 2026 celebrates 140 years of Franco-Korean friendship, a diplomatic and cultural relationship that has united our two nations since 1886. This double commemoration makes our competition much more than a musical event: it becomes the living symbol of an artistic dialogue between East and West.
introduction.content3: At Château de La Ferté-Imbault, from July 5 to 11, 2026, we will welcome the most brilliant lyrical talents from around the world. This historic edition promises to be an exceptional moment, where artistic excellence meets cultural heritage, and where the voices of tomorrow rise in the continuity of a tradition of excellence.

tour.title: The 2024 winners on tour with Sumi Jo
tour.dates:
Concert dates and venues:
June 10, 2025: Shijiazhuang, China
June 12, 2025: Shenzhen, China
June 19, 2025: Jeonju, South Korea
June 21, 2025: Seongnam, South Korea
June 22, 2025: Seoul, South Korea
June 24, 2025: Chuncheon, South Korea
tour.description: Since the competition, the winners have developed their careers with continuous support from Sumi Jo and her team. This exceptional tour has offered them international visibility alongside the world-renowned soprano, including press conferences and television appearances.

harmony.label: An exceptional venue
harmony.title: Le Château de La Ferté-Imbault
harmony.subtitle: OPEN TO ALL VOCAL CATEGORIES AND ALL COUNTRIES WORLDWIDE
harmony.content1: Located in the Loire Valley less than two hours from Paris and built on foundations dating back to 980 AD, the estate of La Ferté-Imbault features a remarkably preserved ensemble from the 16th and 17th centuries, including the main château, the outbuildings, the pavilions, the moats, the double terrace, the gardens and the park. Completed in 1627 and presenting a harmonious composition of architecture and landscape, its design is a manifest synthesis of its history: both a fortress (hence the name Ferté) with an imposing silhouette evocative of a glorious military past, and an elegant château with large windows and sculpted facades revealing all the refinement of the Renaissance spirit and the Age of Enlightenment.

sumijo.title: Sumi Jo
sumijo.subtitle: "The Voice from Heaven"
sumijo.intro: An international icon of opera, Sumi Jo embodies vocal excellence and artistic passion for four decades.

career40.title: A Dazzling 40-Year Career
career40.content: Originally from South Korea, Sumi Jo has established herself as one of the most accomplished sopranos of our time. Her exceptionally rich career is acclaimed by critics and audiences alike for the remarkable musicality of her voice, her agility, precision, and the unique purity of her timbre. She is today one of the classical artists who has sold the most albums in the history of music, with over 50 recordings to her credit, including several platinum albums.

formation.title: Training and International Recognition
formation.content: After initial studies in Korea, Sumi Jo perfected her art at the Conservatorio dell'Accademia di Santa Cecilia in Rome, graduating with honors in 1986. Her meteoric rise was marked by:
items: First prizes in the most prestigious international competitions
A privileged collaboration with the conductor of the century, Herbert von Karajan, who nicknamed her "The Voice from Heaven"
A Grammy Award for her recording of Die Frau ohne Schatten under Sir Georg Solti

repertoire.title: An Exceptionally Broad Repertoire
repertoire.content: Sumi Jo has graced the stages of the world's greatest opera houses, performing iconic roles from the lyric repertoire:
venues: Metropolitan Opera in New York: Lucia di Lammermoor, Gilda (Rigoletto), Olympia (Les Contes d'Hoffmann)
La Scala in Milan: Bel Canto roles such as Le Comte Ory or Fra Diavolo
Wiener Staatsoper: Her memorable debuts as Queen of the Night (The Magic Flute) and Zerbinetta
repertoire.composers: Her repertoire spans from Mozart and Rossini to Donizetti, Bellini, Strauss, and Gounod, even including contemporary works like Nixon in China.

beyond.title: Beyond Opera: Discography and Distinctions
beyond.content: The diversity of her talent is expressed through a varied discography ranging from baroque to American musicals. She was notably nominated for Oscars and Golden Globes for her song Simple Song #3 in Sorrentino's film Youth, where she plays herself.
beyond.honors: Her cultural and humanitarian contributions have earned her prestigious titles:
titles: Commander of the Order of Arts and Letters (France, 2025)
Knight of the Order of the Star of Italy (2019)

transmission.title: Transmission and Vocation: The SJISC
transmission.content: Today, Sumi Jo dedicates herself to passing on her experience to new generations. Through the Sumi Jo International Singing Competition (SJISC), she has created a new kind of competition, based on ambition, emotion, and generosity. Her goal is to offer a springboard to young talents while promoting diversity and inclusion in the world of opera.

event1.description: MISS JO WINS THE GRAMMY AWARD FOR PRINCIPAL SOLOIST IN THE BEST OPERA RECORDING FOR RICHARD STRAUSS' "DIE FRAU OHNE SCHATTEN".

event2.description: SHE RECEIVES FROM MR MATSUURA, DIRECTOR GENERAL OF UNESCO, THE TITLE "UNESCO ARTIST FOR PEACE".

event3.description: CELEBRATING 30 YEARS OF CAREER, SHE PERFORMS CHINESE, AMERICAN, AUSTRALIAN AND EUROPEAN TOURS. THE SONG SHE PERFORMS IN SORRENTINO'S FILM "YOUTH" IS NOMINATED FOR A GOLDEN GLOBE AND AN ACADEMY AWARD.

event4.description: ON JULY 15, SUMI JO OFFICIALLY LAUNCHES THE FIRST INTERNATIONAL SINGING COMPETITION BEARING HER NAME.

festival.title: FESTIVAL & VOCATION
festival.intro: Driven by the desire that her journey and passion can serve to inspire, support and reveal the talents of other artists, Miss Jo creates with the SUMI JO INTERNATIONAL SINGING COMPETITION a new kind of festival closer to the public and artists, based on three key values.

ambition.title: AMBITION
ambition.content: Through different formats, auditions, concerts, masterclasses, the week aims to share musical culture in all its beauty and diversity with as wide an audience as possible.

transmission.title: TRANSMISSION
transmission.content: As Miss Jo herself learned from her extraordinary successive mentors, the sharing of experience and passion is fundamental to perpetuate artistic excellence.

emotion.title: EMOTION
emotion.content: SJISC participants will benefit from an exceptionally romantic and privileged environment conducive to inspiration and artistic excellence.

sponsorship.title: PARTNERS & SPONSORS
sponsorship.intro: Becoming a partner or sponsor of the competition represents a unique opportunity to align your brand with artistic excellence and actively contribute to a cultural event of global scope.

visibility.title: International Visibility
visibility.description: Elevate your brand's prestige internationally. Benefit from targeted media exposure and enhanced brand image through extensive visibility across all our communication platforms.

engagement.title: Loyalty and Engagement
engagement.description: Strengthen your customer and partner relationships. Differentiate your company through artistic excellence and cultural commitment, demonstrating deep engagement in cultural development.

vip.title: Exclusive VIP Experiences
vip.description: Offer your key clients and partners a unique immersion in the world of art: best concert seats, private meetings with world-renowned artists, and access to rehearsals.

networking.title: Media Coverage & Networking
networking.description: Enjoy national and international media coverage. Strategic networking with influential personalities from the cultural world and exclusive events to forge new alliances.

support.title: Support Methods
support.subtitle: Three ways to support us and contribute to the success of this exceptional competition

financial.title: Financial Support
financial.description: Contribute financially to the competition with attractive tax benefits

inKind.title: In-Kind Support
inKind.description: Provide your contribution through services or material goods

skills.title: Skills-Based Support
skills.description: Share your expertise and professional skills

cta.title: Join Us
cta.description: We are at your complete disposal to discuss partnership opportunities and build together a project tailored to your objectives.

venue.title: The association of Château de La Ferté-Imbault
venue.name: Château de La Ferté-Imbault

chateau.title: Château de La Ferté-Imbault
chateau.subtitle: An exceptional setting in the heart of Sologne

description.paragraph1: Nestled in the heart of Sologne, the Château de La Ferté-Imbault is an architectural jewel from the 16th century. This exceptional venue combines the elegance of its Renaissance architecture with the natural beauty of its 30-hectare park, offering a unique setting for international cultural events.
description.paragraph2: Listed as a Historic Monument, the château has been meticulously restored to preserve its authentic character while providing modern infrastructure. Its state rooms, chapel, and French gardens constitute a perfect setting to host the competition and its associated events.

location.title: Location
location.description: 2 hours from Paris, in the heart of Sologne, in a preserved natural setting

history.title: History
history.description: 16th-century historic monument, witness to French history

capacity.title: Capacity
capacity.description: Concert halls, state rooms, and spaces for masterclasses

heritage.title: Heritage
heritage.description: Renaissance architecture and listed French gardens

gardens.title: The Gardens
gardens.description: The château's French gardens offer an enchanting setting for outdoor concerts and moments of conviviality among participants. Carefully restored, they present a perfect harmony between historical heritage and lush nature.
features: Panoramic view of the Sologne ponds

president.name: Sumi Jo
president.bio: World-renowned soprano, Sumi Jo has graced the international opera stage for over 36 years with her exceptional voice. She has performed in the world's greatest venues and has sold over 50 recordings, including several platinum albums. UNESCO Artist for Peace, she was the favorite singer of Maestro Herbert von Karajan and won a Grammy Award for her performance in Richard Strauss' 'Die Frau ohne Schatten'.

operowicz.name: Olivier O. Medinger
operowicz.role:
Jury President
Owner of Château de La Ferté-Imbault
operowicz.bio:
Jury President and owner of Château de La Ferté-Imbault.
Passionate about promoting young talent, he works to create exceptional opportunities for emerging artists in the prestigious setting of Château de La Ferté-Imbault.

friend.name: Jonathan Friend
friend.role: Artistic Advisor to the Houston Grand Opera
friend.bio: Jonathan Friend was born in London, England, and educated in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey. He joined English National Opera in 1974, becoming the Archivist in 1977. In 1979 he became Company Manager for English National Opera North in Leeds. In 1981, he was invited by James Levine to come to the Metropolitan Opera, becoming Company Manager in 1982 and Artistic Administrator in 1984. He remained in that capacity for 36 years, working with two music directors and five general managers, being responsible for season planning and for engaging singers and conductors. He then became Artistic Advisor to the Met from 2019 to 2025, completing a total of 44 years with that company. He was appointed Artistic Advisor to the Irish National Opera in Dublin in 2022, Casting Consultant to the Royal Swedish Opera in Stockholm in 2024, and Casting Consultant to the Houston Grand Opera in 2025. He frequently serves as a judge on vocal competitions.

galoppini.name: Alessandro Galoppini
galoppini.bio: As Casting Manager of the prestigious Teatro alla Scala in Milan, Alessandro Galoppini is at the heart of discovering and selecting the world's finest operatic talents. His experience in one of the world's most demanding opera houses makes him an essential expert.

lanceron.name: Alain Lanceron
lanceron.role: Chairman Emeritus of Warner Classics & Erato
lanceron.bio:
Alain Lanceron, born in Nice in 1949, is a major figure in classical music and international phonographic publishing. A graduate of ESCP, he joined Pathé Marconi-EMI in 1972 and became director of the classical department in 1978. He subsequently held leading positions, including the worldwide presidency of Virgin Classics, then Warner Classics & Erato, which he directed from Paris starting in 2014 before becoming Chairman Emeritus in 2026.

Throughout his career, he has supervised over 1,000 recordings, including numerous world premieres, making a decisive contribution to the rediscovery and dissemination of the French musical heritage. Deeply committed to supporting artists, he has chaired CNIPAL, the Victoires de la Musique Classique, and regularly participates in international competition juries. He is an Officer of Arts and Letters, Knight of the Legion of Honor, and Knight of the National Order of Merit.

rhorer.name: Jérémie Rhorer
rhorer.role: Conductor, composer and director of Le Cercle de l'Harmonie
rhorer.bio: Conductor, composer and artistic director of Le Cercle de l'Harmonie, Jérémie Rhorer is internationally recognized for his innovative approach to baroque and classical music. His profound musical understanding brings an essential artistic dimension to the jury.

vargas.name: Ramón Vargas
vargas.bio: Internationally renowned Mexican tenor, Ramón Vargas has performed on the world's greatest operatic stages. His exceptional career and mastery of bel canto make him an ideal mentor to guide and evaluate young talent.

allemendinger.name: Mélanie Allmendinger
allemendinger.role: Artistic Administrator at the Royal Opera House in London
allemendinger.bio:
Melanie Allmendinger was born in Germany and studied International Arts Management in Freiburg, passing her degree with distinction in 2004. After gaining experience at the Fundación Goethe in Madrid, the Bregenz Festival, and the Bavarian State Opera, she returned to the Bregenz Festival as Company Manager in 2006.

Since November 2006, Melanie has been working at the Royal Opera House, Covent Garden in different roles. First as Assistant and Deputy Company Manager, then as Company Manager and since 2019 as Artistic Administrator of The Royal Opera.

She is a member of the steering group of Opera Europa's Artistic Administration & Producing forum.

In her spare time, she is an active member of the German Choir of London giving her the opportunity to perform in the Sagrada Familia, St Peter's Basilica in Rome, and Cathédrale Notre-Dame in Paris.

gavazzeni.name: Paolo Gavazzeni
gavazzeni.bio:
Born in Bergamo on August 18, 1969, Artistic Director and Opera Director with extensive experience in the musical and theatrical sectors at both national and international levels.

He obtained a Diploma in Piano Performance from the Conservatorio G. Donizetti in Bergamo in 1990 and subsequently attended an advanced piano course at the École Internationale de Piano in Lausanne (1990-1992) under the guidance of Maestro Fausto Zadra. Between 1990 and 1994, he performed in concert as a soloist, in chamber ensembles, and with orchestra.

In 1994, he graduated in Law from the Università degli Studi di Milano, with a thesis on the employment relationship between opera institutions and artistic ensembles.

Professional Experience:
• Artistic Director of Tele+3 Classica (1996-2000)
• Artistic Coordinator at the Accademia Teatro alla Scala (2000-2002)
• Head of Musical Services at the Fondazione Teatro alla Scala (2002-2006)
• Head of Musical Services & Manager of Daily Theatre Operations, Fondazione Teatro alla Scala (2007-2011)
• Artistic Director of the Fondazione Arena di Verona (2012-2016)
• Artistic Director of Classica HD (since 2016) and host of the programme Mestiere Teatro (since 2021)
• Artistic Director of the Macerata Opera Festival (2023-2024)

Since 2016, he has been active as an opera director and, together with Piero Maranghi, has staged productions in major Italian theatres, including: Aida (Teatro Coccia di Novara, October 2016), Manon Lescaut (Teatro Massimo Bellini di Catania, March 2017), Delitto e Dovere (Festival dei Due Mondi di Spoleto, July 2017), Rigoletto (Teatro Coccia di Novara and Teatro Comunale di Sassari, October-November 2018), Le cantatrici villane (Teatro Petrarca di Arezzo, July 2019), Pagliacci (Teatro Comunale di Sassari, October 2019; Teatro Lirico di Cagliari, June 2022), La Cenerentola (Teatro Massimo Bellini di Catania, December 2019), Il tabarro (Teatro Comunale di Sassari, September 2020), Il Trespolo tutore by A. Stradella (Teatro Carlo Felice di Genova, October 2020), La Bohème (Teatro Regio di Torino, March 2020 and February 2022), La Traviata (Teatro Bellini di Catania, March 2021), Adriana Lecouvreur (Teatro Massimo Bellini di Catania, March 2022), La Cenerentola (Teatro Verdi di Trieste, April 2024; Teatro Nuovo Giovanni da Udine, May 2024).

alvarez.name: Marcelo Álvarez
alvarez.bio:
Marcelo Álvarez, born in Córdoba (Argentina), is today one of the most important tenors on the international stage.

After meeting Giuseppe Di Stefano, who immediately recognized his talent, he moved to Europe where he made his debut in La Sonnambula.

His intense and prestigious career has taken him to the world's greatest opera houses, including Teatro alla Scala in Milan, the Metropolitan Opera in New York, the Royal Opera House in London, the Opéra National de Paris, the Wiener Staatsoper, the Teatro Real in Madrid, the Teatro Colón in Buenos Aires, the Bayerische Staatsoper in Munich, and the Staatsoper Unter den Linden in Berlin.

His repertoire is vast and has followed a natural vocal evolution from the bel canto repertoire, through French opera, to the great lyric and verismo repertoire. Among his most significant roles: Edgardo in Lucia di Lammermoor, Alfredo in La Traviata, the Duke of Mantua in Rigoletto, Des Grieux in Manon, Don José in Carmen, Rodolfo in La Bohème, Cavaradossi in Tosca, Manrico in Il Trovatore, Radamès in Aida, Andrea Chénier, Canio in Pagliacci, and Turiddu in Cavalleria Rusticana.

Alongside his operatic career, Marcelo Álvarez has undertaken an intense international recital tour, with performances at the Salle Cortot in Paris, the Ehrbar Saal in Vienna, and the Kammermusiksaal of the Berlin Philharmonie.

Throughout his career, he has collaborated with some of the most distinguished conductors, including James Levine, Zubin Mehta, Lorin Maazel, Claudio Abbado, Riccardo Muti, Riccardo Chailly, and Antonio Pappano.

Marcelo Álvarez's discography includes over thirty recordings with major labels, including Sony and Decca.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: The association of Château de La Ferté-Imbault, organizer of the SUMI JO International Singing Competition (hereinafter "the Competition"), is committed to protecting the privacy and personal data of its users, candidates, partners, and visitors. This privacy policy describes how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal data in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and French data protection law.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.content: The data controller for personal data is:
controller.name: Association du Château de La Ferté-Imbault
controller.address: Château de La Ferté-Imbault, 41300 La Ferté-Imbault, France

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.intro: We collect different types of personal data depending on your interactions with our website and the Competition:

contact.title: Contact data:
contact.item3: Message sent via the contact form

application.title: Application data:
application.item1: Identity information (name, date of birth, nationality)
application.item3: Artistic and musical background (CV, biography, recordings)

purpose.title: Purpose of Processing
purpose.item1: Manage Competition applications and communicate with candidates
purpose.item2: Respond to contact and information requests
purpose.item3: Send information about the Competition (with your consent)
purpose.item4: Manage relationships with our partners and sponsors

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.intro: The processing of your personal data is based on the following legal grounds:
legalBasis.item2: Performance of a contract (for managing applications)
legalBasis.item3: Our legitimate interest (for improving our services and ensuring security)

retention.title: Data Retention Period
retention.content: Your personal data is kept for the time necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected. Candidate data is kept for 5 years after the end of the relevant Competition. Contact data is kept for 3 years after the last contact. Technical data is kept for a maximum of 13 months.

sharing.title: Data Sharing
sharing.intro: Your personal data may be shared with:
sharing.item1: Jury members and the Competition organizing team
sharing.item2: Our technical service providers (hosting, email sending)
sharing.item3: Competent authorities in case of legal obligation

transfers.title: International Transfers
transfers.content: As part of an international competition, some data may be transferred to countries outside the European Union. In such cases, we ensure that appropriate safeguards are in place (standard contractual clauses, adequacy decisions).

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.intro: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

access.title: Right of access:
access.desc: obtain a copy of your personal data

erasure.title: Right to erasure:
erasure.desc: request the deletion of your data

restriction.title: Right to restriction:
restriction.desc: restrict the processing of your data

portability.title: Right to portability:
portability.desc: receive your data in a structured format

objection.title: Right to object:
objection.desc: object to the processing of your data

rights.howTo: To exercise these rights, contact us at contact@sumijo-isc.com. We will respond to your request within one month.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.content: Our website uses technical cookies necessary for its operation and analytical cookies to measure audience (with your consent). You can manage your cookie preferences at any time through your browser settings.

security.title: Data Security
security.content: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, modification, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include data encryption, strict access controls, and regular security audits.

changes.title: Policy Changes
changes.content: We reserve the right to modify this privacy policy at any time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. We encourage you to review this page regularly.

contactSection.title: Contact Us
contactSection.content: For any questions regarding this privacy policy or the processing of your personal data, you can contact us:

cnil.title: Complaint to the CNIL
cnil.content: If you believe that the processing of your personal data constitutes a violation of the GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the French Data Protection Authority (CNIL): www.cnil.fr

editor.title: Website Publisher
editor.name: L'association du château de La Ferté-Imbault
editor.address: Château de La Ferté-Imbault, 41300 La Ferté-Imbault, France

director.title: Publication Director
director.content: The publication director is Mr. Olivier Ojzerowicz-Medinger, in his capacity as President of L'association du château de La Ferté-Imbault.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content1: All content on this website (texts, images, videos, logos, graphics, icons, sounds, software, etc.) is the exclusive property of L'association du château de La Ferté-Imbault or its partners and is protected by French and international intellectual property laws.
intellectualProperty.content2: Any reproduction, representation, modification, publication, or adaptation of all or part of the website elements, regardless of the means or process used, is prohibited without prior written authorization from the association. Any unauthorized use of the website or its content will be considered as counterfeiting and prosecuted in accordance with the provisions of articles L.335-2 and following of the French Intellectual Property Code.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content1: L'association du château de La Ferté-Imbault strives to ensure the accuracy and updating of information published on this website. However, it cannot guarantee the accuracy, precision, or completeness of the information made available on this site.
liability.content2: Consequently, the association declines all responsibility for any inaccuracies, errors, or omissions in the information available on this website. Users acknowledge that they use this information at their own risk.

links.title: Hyperlinks
links.content: This website may contain hyperlinks to other websites. L'association du château de La Ferté-Imbault has no control over these sites and declines all responsibility for their content. The existence of a hyperlink to another site does not constitute validation of that site or its content.

law.title: Applicable Law
law.content: These legal notices are governed by French law. In case of dispute, and after failure of any attempt to find an amicable solution, French courts shall have sole jurisdiction to hear the dispute.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For any questions regarding these legal notices or the website, you can contact us:

semifinalists.subtitle: 24 artists selected from 500 candidates worldwide.
semifinalists.description: Discover the 24 exceptional voices who will perform at the Château de La Ferté-Imbault in July 2026.
